content.full_text:
FAIR p OLITICAL p RACTICES COMMISSION 
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 958 I 4-2329 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
Thomas K. Butt 
City Council Member 
117 Park Place 
Richmond, CA 94801 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our file No. W-11-129 
Dear Mr. Butt: 
August 26, 2011 
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the conflict-of-interest 
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act"). 1 You wrote to request further information 
regarding whether the public generally exception applies. On August 17th, 2011, I wrote to 
inform you that without certain facts, we are unable to provide advice on this exception. I am 
again enclosing a copy of Regulations 18707 and 18707 .1 for your review. You are welcome to 
write in again with the appropriate facts. We are considering this request withdrawn. 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
HMR:jgl 
Enclosure 
Sincerely, 
Zackery P. Morazzini 
General Counsel 
By: 
Heather M. Rowan 
Counsel, Legal Division 
1 The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory 
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

sections.question_synthetic: Whether the public generally exception under the Political Reform Act applies to the requestor's specific circumstances regarding conflict-of-interest provisions.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC is unable to provide advice without certain factual information and is treating the request as withdrawn, inviting the requestor to resubmit with appropriate facts.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a withdrawal letter rather than a standard advice letter. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present. The substantive legal question is inferred from the letter's discussion of the public generally exception and references to Regulations 18707 and 18707.1. The FPPC references a prior letter dated August 17, 2011 that apparently requested additional facts.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether the public generally exception under the Political Reform Act applies to the requestor's specific circumstances regarding conflict-of-interest provisions.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC is unable to provide advice without certain factual information and is treating the request as withdrawn, inviting the requestor to resubmit with appropriate facts.

embedding.summary: The FPPC withdraws its consideration of Thomas K. Butt's request for advice regarding the public generally exception to conflict-of-interest provisions, citing insufficient factual information to render advice.
